---
title: "NIRS in Peripheral Artery Disease"
subtitle: "Differentiating upper and lower limbs and correlation with the ankle-brachial index"
description: "Pilot study evaluating near-infrared spectroscopy as a non-invasive complementary tool for PAD assessment"
date: 2025-08-21
image: cardionir-pad-nirs-banner.svg
categories: [CardioNIR, NIRS, PAD, Machine Learning]
toc: true
toc-depth: 2
page-layout: article
title-block-banner: "#1a2a3a"
title-block-banner-color: white
---

```{=html}
<style>
  .kpi-grid {
    display: grid;
    grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(180px, 1fr));
    gap: 1rem;
    margin: 1.5rem 0 2rem;
  }
  .kpi-card {
    background: var(--bs-tertiary-bg, #f8f9fa);
    border: 1px solid var(--bs-border-color, #dee2e6);
    border-radius: 0.5rem;
    padding: 1.25rem 1rem;
    text-align: center;
    transition: transform 0.2s;
  }
  .kpi-card:hover { transform: translateY(-2px); }
  .kpi-number {
    font-size: 2rem;
    font-weight: 700;
    color: var(--bs-primary, #2c3e50);
    line-height: 1.1;
  }
  .kpi-number .unit {
    font-size: 1rem;
    font-weight: 400;
    opacity: 0.7;
  }
  .kpi-label {
    font-size: 0.78rem;
    color: var(--bs-secondary-color, #6c757d);
    margin-top: 0.35rem;
    line-height: 1.4;
  }

  .finding-grid {
    display: grid;
    grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr;
    gap: 1rem;
    margin: 1.5rem 0;
  }
  @media (max-width: 768px) {
    .finding-grid { grid-template-columns: 1fr; }
  }
  .finding-card {
    border-left: 4px solid var(--bs-primary, #2c3e50);
    background: var(--bs-tertiary-bg, #f8f9fa);
    border-radius: 0 0.5rem 0.5rem 0;
    padding: 1.25rem;
  }
  .finding-card.teal { border-left-color: #18bc9c; }
  .finding-card h4 {
    font-size: 1rem;
    margin-bottom: 0.4rem;
  }
  .finding-card p {
    font-size: 0.88rem;
    margin: 0;
    color: var(--bs-secondary-color, #6c757d);
    line-height: 1.65;
  }

  .pipeline {
    display: flex;
    gap: 0;
    margin: 1.5rem 0;
    overflow-x: auto;
  }
  .pipeline-step {
    flex: 1;
    min-width: 160px;
    padding: 1rem;
    background: var(--bs-tertiary-bg, #f8f9fa);
    border: 1px solid var(--bs-border-color, #dee2e6);
    position: relative;
  }
  .pipeline-step:first-child { border-radius: 0.5rem 0 0 0.5rem; }
  .pipeline-step:last-child { border-radius: 0 0.5rem 0.5rem 0; }
  .pipeline-step .step-num {
    font-size: 0.65rem;
    font-weight: 700;
    text-transform: uppercase;
    letter-spacing: 0.08em;
    color: var(--bs-primary, #2c3e50);
    margin-bottom: 0.4rem;
  }
  .pipeline-step h5 {
    font-size: 0.9rem;
    margin-bottom: 0.3rem;
  }
  .pipeline-step p {
    font-size: 0.78rem;
    color: var(--bs-secondary-color, #6c757d);
    margin: 0;
    line-height: 1.55;
  }
  .pipeline-step:not(:last-child)::after {
    content: '→';
    position: absolute;
    right: -0.55rem;
    top: 50%;
    transform: translateY(-50%);
    z-index: 2;
    font-size: 0.85rem;
    color: var(--bs-secondary-color, #6c757d);
    background: var(--bs-body-bg, #fff);
    width: 1.1rem;
    height: 1.1rem;
    display: flex;
    align-items: center;
    justify-content: center;
    border-radius: 50%;
  }
  @media (max-width: 768px) {
    .pipeline { flex-direction: column; gap: 0.5rem; }
    .pipeline-step { border-radius: 0.5rem !important; }
    .pipeline-step::after { display: none !important; }
  }

  .spectrum-container {
    background: var(--bs-tertiary-bg, #f8f9fa);
    border: 1px solid var(--bs-border-color, #dee2e6);
    border-radius: 0.5rem;
    padding: 1.5rem;
    margin: 1.5rem 0;
  }
  .spectrum-bar {
    height: 40px;
    border-radius: 6px;
    background: linear-gradient(90deg,
      #2d1b4e 0%, #6b2142 12%, #b02a1a 25%, #e07030 38%,
      #e0a830 50%, #8cb030 62%, #30a898 72%, #285e8e 85%, #1a2e4a 100%);
    position: relative;
    margin-bottom: 0.3rem;
  }
  .spectrum-marker {
    position: absolute;
    top: 0;
    height: 100%;
    border: 2px solid rgba(255,255,255,0.85);
    border-radius: 3px;
    background: rgba(255,255,255,0.1);
  }
  .marker-class { left: 0%; width: 20%; }
  .marker-abi { left: 66%; width: 4.5%; }
  .spectrum-ticks {
    display: flex;
    justify-content: space-between;
    font-size: 0.7rem;
    color: var(--bs-secondary-color, #6c757d);
    font-family: monospace;
  }
  .spectrum-legend {
    display: flex;
    gap: 2rem;
    margin-top: 1rem;
    font-size: 0.8rem;
    flex-wrap: wrap;
  }
  .spectrum-legend span {
    display: flex;
    align-items: center;
    gap: 0.4rem;
    color: var(--bs-secondary-color, #6c757d);
  }
  .dot {
    width: 10px;
    height: 10px;
    border-radius: 3px;
    display: inline-block;
  }
  .dot-primary { background: var(--bs-primary, #2c3e50); }
  .dot-teal { background: #18bc9c; }

  .cite-box {
    background: var(--bs-tertiary-bg, #f8f9fa);
    border: 1px solid var(--bs-border-color, #dee2e6);
    border-radius: 0.5rem;
    padding: 1.25rem;
    font-family: monospace;
    font-size: 0.82rem;
    line-height: 1.7;
    margin: 1rem 0;
    color: var(--bs-secondary-color, #6c757d);
    position: relative;
  }
  .cite-copy-btn {
    position: absolute;
    top: 0.6rem;
    right: 0.6rem;
    background: var(--bs-body-bg, #fff);
    border: 1px solid var(--bs-border-color, #dee2e6);
    color: var(--bs-secondary-color, #6c757d);
    padding: 0.25rem 0.6rem;
    border-radius: 4px;
    font-family: monospace;
    font-size: 0.68rem;
    cursor: pointer;
    transition: all 0.2s;
  }
  .cite-copy-btn:hover {
    background: var(--bs-primary, #2c3e50);
    color: white;
    border-color: var(--bs-primary, #2c3e50);
  }

  .team-grid {
    display: grid;
    grid-template-columns: repeat(auto-fit, minmax(220px, 1fr));
    gap: 1rem;
    margin: 1rem 0;
  }
  .team-card {
    background: var(--bs-tertiary-bg, #f8f9fa);
    border: 1px solid var(--bs-border-color, #dee2e6);
    border-radius: 0.5rem;
    padding: 1.1rem;
  }
  .team-card strong { display: block; margin-bottom: 0.15rem; }
  .team-card .affil {
    font-size: 0.78rem;
    color: var(--bs-secondary-color, #6c757d);
  }
  .team-card .credits {
    font-size: 0.72rem;
    font-family: monospace;
    color: var(--bs-secondary-color, #6c757d);
    margin-top: 0.5rem;
    line-height: 1.5;
  }
</style>
```

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
**Published in** *Microvascular Research* 162, 104864 (2025) — [doi:10.1016/j.mvr.2025.104864](https://doi.org/10.1016/j.mvr.2025.104864) — Open Access (CC BY 4.0)
:::

## At a Glance

```{=html}
<div class="kpi-grid">
  <div class="kpi-card">
    <div class="kpi-number">0.91</div>
    <div class="kpi-label">AUC-ROC for limb<br>differentiation (95% CI 0.88–0.94)</div>
  </div>
  <div class="kpi-card">
    <div class="kpi-number">51</div>
    <div class="kpi-label">PAD patients<br>102 lower limbs evaluated</div>
  </div>
  <div class="kpi-card">
    <div class="kpi-number">1429<span class="unit"> nm</span></div>
    <div class="kpi-label">Start of optimal wavelength<br>for ABI prediction</div>
  </div>
  <div class="kpi-card">
    <div class="kpi-number">0.167</div>
    <div class="kpi-label">R² NIRS–ABI correlation<br>(RMSECV = 0.186)</div>
  </div>
</div>
```

## Background

Peripheral artery disease (PAD) affects over 230 million adults worldwide, yet remains frequently underdiagnosed. The ankle-brachial index (ABI) is the standard first-line test, but has known limitations — particularly in patients with arterial calcification, diabetes, or chronic renal disease, where falsely elevated values can mask compromised flow.

Near-infrared spectroscopy (NIRS) offers a non-invasive, portable alternative that penetrates superficial tissues to assess hemoglobin oxygenation and tissue composition. Despite its established role in cerebral oximetry, its application to PAD remains underexplored. This pilot study investigated whether NIRS spectral signatures can differentiate ischemic from non-ischemic limbs and whether they carry information correlated with ABI — all using a portable spectrometer at rest in a routine outpatient setting.

## Key Findings

```{=html}
<div class="finding-grid">
  <div class="finding-card">
    <h4>🔴 Strong Limb Discrimination</h4>
    <p>Random Forest classification achieved an AUC of 0.91, with the most discriminative wavelengths below 1060 nm and in the 1250–1375 nm range — consistent with oxygenated hemoglobin absorption peaks.</p>
  </div>
  <div class="finding-card teal">
    <h4>🟢 ABI Spectral Correlation</h4>
    <p>iPLS identified a narrow band at 1429–1463 nm as the most informative for ABI prediction. This region captures O–H stretching (water), N–H stretching (proteins), and CH₂ combination bands (lipids) — pointing to vascular tissue composition signatures.</p>
  </div>
  <div class="finding-card">
    <h4>📡 Portable & At-Rest Protocol</h4>
    <p>Unlike previous NIRS–PAD studies requiring exercise testing, we demonstrated feasibility at rest in an outpatient clinic, using a compact InGaAs-based spectrometer (900–1700 nm) with no cooling requirements.</p>
  </div>
  <div class="finding-card teal">
    <h4>🔗 Complementary to ABI</h4>
    <p>NIRS may address ABI limitations in patients with non-compressible vessels. The modest R² was expected — the goal was not to replicate ABI but to identify whether NIRS carries independent hemodynamic information.</p>
  </div>
</div>
```

## Spectral Windows

The most discriminative wavelengths for limb classification and ABI prediction fall in **distinct** regions of the NIR spectrum, reflecting different physiological information:

```{=html}
<div class="spectrum-container">
  <div class="spectrum-bar">
    <div class="spectrum-marker marker-class"></div>
    <div class="spectrum-marker marker-abi"></div>
  </div>
  <div class="spectrum-ticks">
    <span>900 nm</span>
    <span>1100</span>
    <span>1300</span>
    <span>1500</span>
    <span>1700 nm</span>
  </div>
  <div class="spectrum-legend">
    <span><span class="dot dot-primary"></span> Classification: OxyHb absorption (900–1060 nm)</span>
    <span><span class="dot dot-teal"></span> ABI prediction: O–H, N–H, CH₂ bands (1429–1463 nm)</span>
  </div>
</div>
```

The 900–1060 nm window aligns with the absorption characteristics of oxygenated hemoglobin (typically peaking between 800–1000 nm), while the 1429–1463 nm region captures molecular signatures from water (O–H), proteins (N–H), and lipids (CH₂) — potentially reflecting vascular tissue composition and perfusion status rather than oxygenation alone.

## Methods

```{=html}
<div class="pipeline">
  <div class="pipeline-step">
    <div class="step-num">Step 01</div>
    <h5>NIRS Acquisition</h5>
    <p>20 scans per site (right thumb, both halluces), 900–1700 nm, 225 data points. Supine, at rest.</p>
  </div>
  <div class="pipeline-step">
    <div class="step-num">Step 02</div>
    <h5>Pre-processing</h5>
    <p>Standard Normal Variate (SNV) transformation for scatter correction and baseline normalisation.</p>
  </div>
  <div class="pipeline-step">
    <div class="step-num">Step 03</div>
    <h5>Classification</h5>
    <p>Random Forest (upper vs. lower limb), 2/3–1/3 split preserving within-patient measurements.</p>
  </div>
  <div class="pipeline-step">
    <div class="step-num">Step 04</div>
    <h5>Regression</h5>
    <p>Interval PLS (iPLS) for wavelength selection and ABI prediction across 21 spectral intervals.</p>
  </div>
</div>
```

### Study Design

This was an observational, cross-sectional study employing a convenience sample of 51 outpatients from the Angiology and Vascular Surgery service at Unidade de Saúde Local de São João, Porto. Patients with PAD (Rutherford 0–3, ABI ≤ 0.90 or abnormal Doppler) were recruited between November 2023 and May 2024. The study received approval from the Ethics Committee (ref. CE-382-24).

### Cohort Summary

| Characteristic | Value |
|:---|:---|
| **N** | 51 patients (102 lower limbs) |
| **Age** | 65.5 ± 8.3 years |
| **Sex** | 86.3% male |
| **Rutherford 1–3** | 94.1% |
| **Median ABI** | 0.65 (IQR 0.4) |
| **Smoking** | 94.1% (37.2% current) |
| **Hypertension** | 72.5% |
| **Diabetes** | 27.5% |
| **Coronary artery disease** | 23.5% |
| **Claudication (limb-level)** | 66% |
| **No palpable distal pulse** | 84.6% |
| **Monophasic / occlusion (Doppler)** | 64.1% |

: {.striped .hover}

### NIRS Device

Spectroscopic measurements used a portable NIR reflectance system (NIR-S-G1) with an InGaAs detector covering 900–1700 nm. The device requires no cooling, holds FCC and CE certifications, and allows rapid spectral acquisition — making it suitable for bedside and outpatient use.

## Limitations & Future Directions

This pilot study has important limitations: a relatively small convenience sample, a predominantly male cohort limited to Rutherford stages 1–3, and the absence of patients with non-compressible vessels (ABI > 1.3) — a subgroup where NIRS could have particular clinical value. The assumption that upper limbs were non-ischemic was based on clinical evaluation without formal hemodynamic confirmation.

Future work should prioritise larger and more diverse cohorts, matched healthy controls, integration of exercise or provocative protocols (e.g., transient leg elevation), and comparison with complementary modalities such as TcPO₂. Standardised guidelines for NIRS sensor placement and acquisition parameters are also essential for cross-study comparability.

## Team

```{=html}
<div class="team-grid">
  <div class="team-card">
    <strong>Jéssica Braga Amorim *†</strong>
    <div class="affil">RISE-Health, Dept. Surgery & Physiology, FMUP</div>
    <div class="credits">Conceptualization · Investigation · Writing (original draft) · Visualization · Validation · Data curation · Methodology</div>
  </div>
  <div class="team-card">
    <strong>Marina Dias Neto †</strong>
    <div class="affil">RISE-Health, FMUP · Angiology & Vascular Surgery, USL São João</div>
    <div class="credits">Conceptualization · Investigation · Supervision · Writing (review & editing) · Visualization · Validation · Data curation · Methodology</div>
  </div>
  <div class="team-card">
    <strong>Sandra Magalhães</strong>
    <div class="affil">RISE-Health, Dept. Surgery & Physiology, FMUP</div>
    <div class="credits">Investigation · Methodology · Data curation</div>
  </div>
  <div class="team-card">
    <strong>António S. Barros</strong>
    <div class="affil">RISE-Health, Dept. Surgery & Physiology, FMUP</div>
    <div class="credits">Conceptualization · Formal analysis · Software · Funding acquisition · Project administration · Supervision · Resources · Writing (review & editing) · Visualization · Validation · Investigation · Methodology · Data curation</div>
  </div>
</div>
<p style="font-size: 0.78rem; color: var(--bs-secondary-color, #6c757d);">* Corresponding author &ensp; † Equal contribution</p>
```

## Citation

```{=html}
<div class="cite-box" id="citation-box">
  <button class="cite-copy-btn" onclick="
    navigator.clipboard.writeText(document.getElementById('cite-text').textContent.trim()).then(() => {
      this.textContent = 'Copied!';
      setTimeout(() => this.textContent = 'Copy', 2000);
    })
  ">Copy</button>
  <span id="cite-text">Braga Amorim J, Dias Neto M, Magalhães S, Barros AS. Pilot study on near-infrared spectroscopy in peripheral artery disease: Differentiating upper and lower limbs and its correlation with the ankle-brachial index. Microvasc Res. 2025;162:104864. doi: 10.1016/j.mvr.2025.104864</span>
</div>
```

### BibTeX

```bibtex
@article{BragaAmorim2025,
  title   = {Pilot study on near-infrared spectroscopy in peripheral
             artery disease: {Differentiating} upper and lower limbs
             and its correlation with the ankle-brachial index},
  author  = {Braga Amorim, J{\'e}ssica and Dias Neto, Marina and
             Magalh{\~a}es, Sandra and Barros, Ant{\'o}nio S.},
  journal = {Microvascular Research},
  volume  = {162},
  pages   = {104864},
  year    = {2025},
  doi     = {10.1016/j.mvr.2025.104864}
}
```

## Funding

This research was supported by Fundação para a Ciência e Tecnologia (FCT) under the **CardioNIR** project — *CARDIOvascular Near-InfraRed spectroscopy probing* — 2021 (PTDC/EMD-EMD/3822/2021).

::: {.callout-tip appearance="simple"}
[doi:10.54499/PTDC/EMD-EMD/3822/2021](https://doi.org/10.54499/PTDC/EMD-EMD/3822/2021)
:::
